patch.value: Enhancement set for pm-slack-standup: (1) --format blocks alias for Slack Block Kit JSON; (2) --schedule option (HH:MM daily or 5-field cron) deferring the post to the next fire time; (3) --include-blockers flag highlighting blocked rows with a 🚨 marker; (4) --team flag filtering the standup by assignee; (5) --compact mode rendering a one-line-per-section short standup. Added pure helpers parseTeam/parseSchedule/nextFireTime with unit tests. Updated flag descriptions, examples, and README. Pending dependency updates (pm-cli & pm-changelog -> 2026.7.6) committed.
Flags implemented and unit-tested; npm test + npm run check pass; CHANGELOG regenerated; not pushed.
Closed at creation via pm create

value.author: codex
value.text:
PR #22 finishing pass (recipe 2026-07-10):

VERIFIED:
- Reproduced the failing CI check: the 'test' job failed on step 'Verify generated changelog' (NOT unit tests — unit tests pass: 84/84). Root cause: CHANGELOG.md was out of date / flattened.
- Fixed by regenerating with 'npm run changelog:full' (pm-changelog@latest); 'npm run changelog:check' now passes. Historical release sections are properly segmented per version (2026.7.7 ... 2026.5.26).
- Unit tests: 91 pass / 0 fail (84 original + 7 new regression tests for the review fixes).
- Functional checks in a throwaway workspace (/tmp/fx-pm-slack-standup): pm init, local install, 'timeout 20 pm --help' returns 0 (no fork-bomb/hang). Exercised --format blocks, --schedule (daily + cron, dry-run), --include-blockers --include-done, --compact --include-blockers, --team alice/bob. Default (no new flags) output is byte-identical to origin/main — defaults unchanged. Malformed cron (-5, 0-, /5) rejected with clean USAGE CommandError exit code 2 (no stack traces). Post without webhook exits 2 with a clean actionable error.

FIXED (bot review findings):
1. Greptile P1 (conf 4/5 main concern): --schedule fetched+built standup data BEFORE the wait, so a scheduled non-dry-run post used a stale snapshot. Moved the fetch/build/compare AFTER the schedule wait, so posts reflect state at fire time.
2. Greptile/gemini/cubic: isBlockerItem did not exclude done statuses — a closed item with a stale blocked_by got a 🚨 marker under --include-done --include-blockers. Added a DONE_STATUSES guard.
3. Greptile/cubic: compactSectionLine dropped the 🚨 marker in --compact --include-blockers (README says blockers highlighted in every format). Passed opts into compactSectionLine and prefixed blocked titles with 🚨.
4. cubic P1: --schedule used a single setTimeout with an unbounded delay; Node clamps >2^31-1 ms (~24.8 days) to 1 ms, firing immediately for sparse cron. Replaced with a chunked sleep loop (<=24-day chunks).
5. gemini/cubic: cron parser used Number() on sliced strings; Number('')===0 accepted -5 (lo=0), 0- (hi=0), /5 (base=0). Added /^\d+$/ validation for range bounds and step base before conversion.

COULD NOT FUNCTIONALLY VERIFY:
- The >24.8-day setTimeout overflow fix: cannot wait days in a test. Verified by code review + the chunked-sleep loop logic; unit tests cover the cron parsing that produces far-future fire times.

DECISIONS:
- All Greptile/cubic/gemini findings addressed as legitimate. No finding refuted.
